title: Sorbus aria var. edulis
what_description: Sorbus aria var. edulis is a fruiting form of the whitebeam tree, a deciduous shrub or small tree in the family Rosaceae. The whitebeam tree has a rounded crown, grey bark, and ovate to lanceolate leaves with 3-5 pairs of lobes, 5-10 cm long, with a pointed tip and serrated margins. The leaves are dark green on top and pale green on the underside, and are arranged alternately on the branches. The whitebeam tree produces white flowers in May, followed by edible, red to purple berries 1-2 cm in diameter, each containing 1-3 seeds. The berries are a key food source for birds and small mammals, and are also consumed by humans.
ailments_description: The most common ailments you can heal with Sorbus aria var. edulis are inflammation, digestive issues, and gastroenteritis, which are alleviated by its anthraquinones and flavonoids that reduce inflammation and alleviate gastrointestinal symptoms. Diarrhea and fever are also treated with Sorbus aria var. edulis due to its antimicrobial properties that inhibit the growth of pathogens causing these conditions. Rheumatism and arthritis are managed by its phenolic compounds that reduce pain and inflammation in joints. The plant's flavonoids also help in treating wounds by accelerating healing and reducing the risk of infection. Additionally, respiratory problems such as cough, asthma, and respiratory issues are treated with Sorbus aria var. edulis due to its flavonoids and phenolic compounds that reduce inflammation and improve lung function.
properties_description: The main therapeutic properties of Sorbus aria var. edulis are its antioxidant activity, which is attributed to the presence of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and ascorbic acid, that protect cells from oxidative stress and damage. The anti-inflammatory properties of this plant are due to the action of saponins and sesquiterpenes, which inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, thereby reducing inflammation and pain. Sorbus aria var. edulis also exhibits antimicrobial activity, which is attributed to the presence of volatile oils, particularly limonene and beta-pinene, that have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi. This plant has cardioprotective effects, as its bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, have been shown to reduce blood pressure, improve lipid profiles, and prevent cardiovascular disease. Furthermore, the analgesic properties of Sorbus aria var. edulis are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to reduce pain by inhibiting the production of pain-related neurotransmitters.
constituents_description: The most important healing constituents of Sorbus aria var. edulis are flavonoids, tannins, phenolic acids, quercetin, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The flavonoids, particularly quercetin, possess potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, scavenging free radicals and inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes. Tannins and phenolic acids also exhibit antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, while saponins have been reported to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and immunomodulatory effects. Quercetin is a key flavonoid responsible for the anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities of Sorbus aria var. edulis, with studies demonstrating its ability to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes. The combined action of these constituents makes Sorbus aria var. edulis a valuable resource for the prevention and treatment of various diseases, including inflammation, oxidative stress, and microbial infections.
preparations_description: The main herbal preparations of Sorbus aria var. edulis are tincture, made by extracting the bark and leaves with a solvent to create a concentrated liquid, tea, prepared by infusing the bark and leaves in hot water, infusion, where the bark and leaves are steeped in cold water to extract their properties, salve, a topical application made by infusing the bark and leaves in a carrier oil to treat skin conditions, and bark decoction, a liquid extract made by boiling the bark in water, which is used for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, due to the presence of compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, that have been shown to exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities.
side_effects_description: The possible side effect of using Sorbus aria var. edulis are gastrointestinal disturbances, including inducing vomiting, inducing nausea and causes diarrhea due to the presence of amygdalin, a compound that releases cyanide when ingested. This can lead to cyanide poisoning, causing rapid heart rate, dizziness, and headaches. In severe cases, it can cause more serious symptoms such as respiratory failure, coma, and even death. The improper use of Sorbus aria var. edulis can also cause stomach cramps and diarrhea, making it a potentially life-threatening situation if not treated promptly. The risk of side effects is higher when the plant is ingested in large quantities or combined with other substances that release cyanide.
